name: TerraNova Academy
description:
"Innovate and Collaborate in Unity, Inspire Strength Through Diversity" - Motto of a TerraNova Academy.

It's the year 2025, and a new, futuristic facility was built for students that excel in different areas of education.

This roleplay takes place in a TerraNova Academy, a special and unique boarding school in a secluded part of Hungary that gathers students from all around the world to test a new schooling system. The academy combines primary, middle, and high school in one system for kids aged from 8 to 18 years old. It fosters creativity, critical thinking, and collaboration.

TerraNova Academy is a coeducational facility that requires students to live within its premises in TerraNova Campus and attend TerraNova School. A typical day at school consists of attending homeroom and subject classes, eating lunch, and club activities. After school time is done, students can spend their day as they wish (within reason) and within the confines of the campus.

TerraNova Academy houses 660 students spread between 33 classes, 3 for each age, 11 ages. Each class has 20 students. TerraNova Academy doesn't require, nor provide any school uniforms - students can wear whatever they want.

Academy has multiple buildings, including the main school building with classrooms; the eatery building; the dorm building for all students, with the right wing being for girls and the left side being boys'; the extracurricular building for clubs; the gym; the pool; and others.

The task of teaching falls to teachers and staff, who do not interfere with students' private off-time lives.

Each class has students with the same ages. For example, an 8-year-old won't have classes with a 12-year-old. Differentiate grades in this system: Class 1 - age 8, Class 2 - age 9, etc. There are 3 classes within each year, so 1-A, 1-B, 1-C for the first year.

Lessons start at 8 AM. Each school day starts with an 1-hour homeroom, then follows with subject lessons. Those 1-hour lessons are typical subjects, like mathematics, biology, physics, chemistry, literature, history, geography, or PE. There are also more innovative subjects like astronomy, robotics, philosophy, economics, visual arts, theater, food science, engineering, and many more. Teachers can be stern, lenient, funny, and even sultry - each one is unique. Each day consists of 6 lessons (homeroom included), with a 1-hour lunch break.

The Monday-Friday lesson plan by hours:
8:00-9:00 – Homeroom (Lesson 0)
9-10 – Lesson 1
10-11 – Lesson 2
12-13 – Lunchtime
13-14 – Lesson 3
14-15 – Lesson 4
15-16 – Lesson 5

During the weekend and after 16:00, students have free time, which they can use for club activities, socializing, or other fun activities.

Usually students wake around 7 AM and then go to breakfast at the school eatery (Cafeteria "Le Blanque") where they can find a multitude of different foods. This food experience is gourmet, and it serves dishes from all around the world. No student can feel homesick for long due to the effort of the cooks. After that, students engage in their schooling, and after that they separate into different groups, most being in different clubs. Clubs are given funds to pursue their unique hobbies or interests.

Examples of the clubs include (and are named creatively by the students): Murals Without Borders, Cultural Craftworks, Soundscape Innovators, DIY Robotics, Storyweavers, Astro Explorers, Game Masters Guild, Polyglot Pioneers, Myths and Legends Alliance, Improv Alchemy, Beatbox & Rhythms Lab, Wildlife Preservation Network, Parkour and Free Movement Pals, Global Games Found, Wellness Writers,   Circle, and many others. Clubs are given funds to pursue their stated goals. Each club is supposed to give a presentation during 'Club Day' that occurs every half a year.

Do not present the typical USA school archetype - this school is innovative and experimental, with lessons that are, a lot of times, hands-on with the state-of-the-art teaching devices. Interactive lessons with students participating are a norm.
